info.name: RBAC – User Permissions (/admin/rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions)
info.description:
E2E tests for the user direct-permission grant API.

Routes covered:
  GET    /api/v1/admin/rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions
  POST   /api/v1/admin/rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions
  DELETE /api/v1/admin/rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions/{grant_id}

Folder execution order:
  1. setup            — register + Gmail OTP + verify-email + login
                        (captures up_access_token — plain user, no RBAC permissions yet)
                        also registers a second target user whose grants are managed
  2. auth-guard       — 401 on all three routes with no Authorization header
  3. rbac-guard       — 403 on all three routes with valid JWT but no permissions
  4. owner-bootstrap  — POST /owner/bootstrap promotes the acting user to owner
                        (owner role grants rbac:read + rbac:grant_user_perm; RBAC middleware
                        reads DB per request — no re-login needed)
  5. happy-path       — full lifecycle: GET (empty) → capture perm → POST grant → GET (one
                        entry; capture up_grant_id) → DELETE revoke → GET (empty again)
  6. not-found        — DELETE unknown grant → 404 grant_not_found
                        POST to invalid (non-UUID) user_id → 404 user_not_found
                        POST with valid role but unknown permission_id → 422 permission_not_found
  7. validation       — 422/400/413 input-error cases for POST
  8. conflict         — 409 permission_already_granted when grant already exists

Guard order (handler):
  GET    /rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions             : JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:read)            → handler
  POST   /rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions             : JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:grant_user_perm) → handler
  DELETE /rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions/{grant_id}  : JWTAuth → RBAC.Require(rbac:grant_user_perm) → handler

The admin router applies AllowContentType("application/json") before JWTAuth, so POST
requests in auth-guard must carry Content-Type: application/json.

Environment variables required:
  bootstrap_secret   — must match BOOTSTRAP_SECRET in .env
  gmail_test_email   — Gmail address for OTP receipt
  gmail_access_token — OAuth2 token for Gmail API reads

Notes on what is NOT covered here (covered by unit tests):
  - DB / store errors → 500                          — handler_test.go, service_test.go
  - ErrPrivilegeEscalation → 403                    — store_test.go (owner holds all perms;
                                                       not testable via black-box e2e)
  - ErrScopeNotAllowed → 422 scope_not_allowed       — store_test.go (depends on seed
                                                       scope_policy; not black-box verifiable)
  - UUID parse failure in service (bad user_id)      — service_test.go (covered separately)
  - nil permissions marshals as [] not null           — handler_test.go

Run `make _e2e-db-clean` and `make _e2e-kv-clean` before each suite run.
Requires TRUSTED_PROXIES=127.0.0.1/32 in .env.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so admin-route requests never collide on any shared bucket.

item.name: setup
item.description:
Registers two users:
  - Acting user (gmail_test_email): will be bootstrapped as owner and act as the caller for all RBAC operations.
  - Target user: a second account whose direct permissions will be granted / revoked.

Both users are registered and verified. The acting user logs in and captures up_access_token. The target user's ID is extracted from their login response and stored as up_target_user_id.

console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for target user verification email...');

if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching email found for target user.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }

item.name: POST /login (target user) → 200 (capture up_target_user_id)
item.description: Target user email is now verified. Logs in and captures up_target_user_id from the JWT sub claim.

exec: console.warn('[WARN] Could not extract target user ID from JWT sub.');

item.name: auth-guard
item.description: Verifies that the JWTAuth middleware rejects requests with no Authorization header before RBAC or handler logic is reached. Tests one request per HTTP method (GET, POST, DELETE). POST carries Content-Type: application/json so the AllowContentType router middleware passes before JWTAuth fires.

exec: // JWTAuth fires first — no token means 401 before RBAC check.
// JWTAuth fires next — no token → 401 before RBAC or body decode.

item.name: rbac-guard
item.description: Verifies that RBAC.Require rejects a valid JWT belonging to a plain user who has not yet been bootstrapped. Must return 403 before any handler logic is called. owner-bootstrap runs after this folder.

exec: // JWTAuth passes (valid token). RBAC.Require(rbac:read) fires next — user has no owner role → 403.
// JWTAuth passes. RBAC.Require(rbac:grant_user_perm) fires next → 403 before body decode.

item.name: owner-bootstrap
item.description: Promotes the acting user to owner by calling POST /owner/bootstrap with the correct bootstrap_secret. The owner role carries all permissions including rbac:read and rbac:grant_user_perm. RBAC.Require reads the DB on every request — no re-login is needed; the existing up_access_token is sufficient for all subsequent requests.

item.name: POST /owner/bootstrap → 201 (grant owner role to acting user)

exec: console.error('[ERROR] up_access_token not set — setup/POST /login did not complete.');
console.error('[ERROR] bootstrap_secret not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
console.log('[INFO] Acting user is now owner — RBAC.Require(rbac:read) and RBAC.Require(rbac:grant_user_perm) will pass on subsequent requests.');

item.name: happy-path
item.description:
Full lifecycle for direct permission grants authenticated as owner.

Execution order:
  1. GET  /admin/permissions                  — capture up_perm_id (first permission in list)
  2. GET  permissions (target, empty)          → 200 with empty permissions array
  3. POST grant permission to target           → 201; assert body shape; capture up_grant_id
  4. GET  permissions (target, one entry)      → 200; assert entry matches grant
  5. DELETE revoke permission from target      → 204
  6. GET  permissions (target, empty again)    → 200 with empty permissions array

// 204 No Content — no body, no Set-Cookie.
pm.test('permissions list is empty after revoke', () => {

item.name: not-found
item.description:
Verifies 404 and 422 cases for unknown IDs.

- DELETE with an unknown grant_id → 404 grant_not_found (RevokePermission: store finds no matching row)
- POST to a user_id that is not a valid UUID → 404 user_not_found (service guard 1: parseID fails → ErrGrantNotFound)
- POST with a valid UUID permission_id that doesn't exist in DB → 422 permission_not_found

exec: // The grant was already revoked in happy-path or never existed — ErrGrantNotFound → 404.

item.name: POST /rbac/users/{unknown_perm}/permissions — unknown permission_id → 422 permission_not_found
item.description: Uses the real target user so the service proceeds past user-parse, but supplies a permission_id UUID that does not exist in the DB. The store returns ErrPermissionNotFound which maps to 422.

item.name: GET /rbac/users/{unknown_user_id}/permissions — user has no grants → 200 empty
item.description: A valid UUID that resolves to no user still returns 200 with an empty permissions array. The service only returns 404 on UUID parse failure; a valid UUID that finds no grants returns [].

item.name: validation
item.description:
Body decode and field validation cases for POST /rbac/users/{user_id}/permissions. All requests carry a valid JWT. Validation fires after RBAC.Require but before any service or DB call — no state is mutated.

Rules from validators.go (validateGrantPermission):
  permission_id required (non-empty after trim)
  granted_reason required (non-empty after trim)
  expires_at required (must be present and non-zero)
  expires_at must be in the future

exec: // validateGrantPermission trims permission_id before checking emptiness.
// expires_at omitted (null pointer) — in.ExpiresAt.IsZero() == true → ErrExpiresAtRequired.

item.name: POST — expires_at in the past → 422 expires_at_in_past

exec: pm.request.body.update({ mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ permission_id: 'any', granted_reason: 'test', expires_at: '2099-01-01T00:00:00Z', _pad: pad }) });

item.name: conflict
item.description:
Verifies that ErrPermissionAlreadyGranted maps to 409 permission_already_granted when granting a permission that is already active for the target user.

Steps:
  1. Grant the permission to the target user.
  2. Attempt to grant the same permission again → 409.
  3. Revoke the grant to leave the DB clean.

item.name: POST — grant permission (setup for conflict test)

exec: if (!permID) { console.error('[ERROR] up_perm_id not set — happy-path/GET /admin/permissions did not complete.'); }
console.log('[INFO] Conflict setup grant created:', pm.response.json().id);
// An active (non-expired) grant already exists for (user, permission) — store returns ErrPermissionAlreadyGranted → 409.
if (!grantID) { console.error('[ERROR] up_conflict_grant_id not set — conflict setup grant did not capture the ID.'); }
pm.test('status is 204 (conflict grant revoked, DB clean)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(204));

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique IP by the collection prerequest.
Set from gmail_test_email. Used by setup/register and login for the acting user.
Password for the acting user. Set in setup prerequest.
6-digit OTP fetched from Gmail during setup/register. Consumed by setup/verify-email.
JWT access_token from setup/login (acting user). Plain user before owner-bootstrap; owner after. Reused for all subsequent folders without re-login.
User ID of the acting user extracted from the JWT sub claim. Stored for reference.
Derived email for the target user (gmail+uptarget variant). Set in setup prerequest.
6-digit OTP fetched from Gmail during setup/register for the target user. Consumed by setup/verify-email (target).
User ID of the target user. Captured from the target user's JWT sub claim. Used as the {user_id} path parameter for all permission-grant requests.
Permission ID captured from GET /admin/permissions[0].id. Used as permission_id in POST requests.
Grant ID (UserPermission.id) captured from the happy-path POST response. Used as {grant_id} for DELETE.
ISO 8601 timestamp 30 days in the future. Set dynamically in the happy-path POST prerequest.
ISO 8601 timestamp 30 days in the future. Set dynamically in the not-found POST prerequest.
ISO 8601 timestamp 30 days in the future. Set dynamically in the conflict POST prerequest.
Grant ID created during the conflict/setup step. Revoked in the conflict/cleanup step.
